channel.title: The Git Reporter
channel.description: A code-native newspaper of meaningful software change.

item.title: Hermes Makes Yolo Obey User Deny Rules
item.description: Hermes added `approvals.deny` on July 5, giving users glob rules that block terminal commands even when the agent is running in yolo or approvals-off mode.

item.title: API Discovery: Recherche d'entreprises Turns Company Search Into Agent Triage
item.description: API Recherche d'entreprises exposes public French company search through text, SIREN/SIRET, geography, NAF, and employee-size filters.

item.title: Builder Work: Mycelium Makes Agents Prove The Product Before Coding
item.description: Haabe's Mycelium is an open-source Claude Code plugin that was publicly described as built using itself and repo-inspected as a decision framework with receipts, git-stored state, and agent skills.

item.title: Pi Turns Issue Analysis Into A Shareable Session Trail
item.description: Pi's July 6 commits changed issue analysis from a CI artifact into a staff-triggered, gist-backed session that can be summarized in public and imported back into a local checkout.

item.title: OpenCode Turns OpenAPI Into Agent Tool Contracts
item.description: OpenCode merged a CodeMode OpenAPI adapter on July 4, giving hosts a way to turn API specs into model-visible tools while keeping credentials and unsupported operations outside the agent's reach.

item.title: API Discovery: API Association Turns Nonprofit Identity Into Agent Checks
item.description: API Associations exposes French nonprofit identity data from the RNA and Sirene sources, giving agents a public way to verify an association before drafting eligibility, grant, or onboarding work.

item.title: Builder Work: Scopewalker Gives Agents A Complexity Gauge
item.description: Timo Haanpaa's Scopewalker MCP is worth studying because an AI-assisted build turns coding standards into read-only measurements an agent can call before it edits.

item.title: OpenClaw Makes Session Threads A Maintainer Problem
item.description: Peter Steinberger's OpenClaw session-management PR turns chat history from a recency list into a lifecycle surface with pinning, archiving, read-only state, and proof screenshots.

item.title: OpenWork Makes Team Memory A Governed Agent Surface
item.description: OpenWork's July 2 Memory Bank merge turns saved agent context into a user-scoped, searchable capability with tests, schema checks, and explicit no-secrets guidance.

item.title: API Discovery: API Sirene Turns Company Identity Into Agent Checks
item.description: INSEE's API Sirene open data gives agents a public way to check French company and establishment identity, with SIREN/SIRET scope and diffusion caveats close to the source.

item.title: Builder Work: KISS Sorcar Turns Self-Hosting Into Agent Discipline
item.description: Koushik Sen's KISS Sorcar is not interesting because it uses agents; it is interesting because the public repo turns self-hosting pressure into invariants, worktrees, parallel agents, and reproducible checks.

item.title: Tau Keeps Branch Context For Contributors
item.description: A July 3 Tau PR from Rian Dolphin turns branch summaries into a contributor-facing safeguard: the next branch should remember why the previous one existed.

item.title: OpenCode Turns Agent Review Into A First-Class Work Surface
item.description: A July 2 review-panel overhaul moves OpenCode's agent output closer to a readable control surface for changed files, comments, media, and stability checks.

item.title: API Discovery: API Adresse Turns French Addresses Into Agent Checks
item.description: API.gouv's API Adresse page exposes a fully open geocoding source that agents can use to validate French addresses before routing, mapping, or form decisions.

item.title: Builder Work: 143 Turns Coding Agents Into Team Infrastructure
item.description: Assembled's open-source 143 project teaches a practical builder lesson: make agent work reviewable by routing it through durable sessions, sandboxes, previews, and normal pull requests.

item.title: OpenWork Reverts Its Provider Wizard To Hold Dev Stable
item.description: Ben's July 1 revert turns a feature rollback into a useful maintainer story: the project tried a provider probe and model picker, then pulled them back before the dev branch carried the risk.

item.title: OpenClaw Lets Agents Wake After A Command Exits
item.description: OpenClaw added an on-exit cron schedule so a gateway-owned watcher can wake an agent after a watched process finishes.

item.title: API Discovery: API Impot Particulier Turns Tax Proof Into An Agent Check
item.description: France's API.gouv catalog exposes a DGFiP tax-data API where agents must treat authorization and proportionality as the product, not an afterthought.

item.title: Codex Gives Subagents A Delivery Receipt
item.description: A Codex commit adds structured send and receive logs for multi-agent communication, making delegation easier to trace without making it today's lead.

item.title: Mario Zechner Makes Pi Sessions Render Their Own Evidence
item.description: Pi's latest extension change lets durable session entries carry custom rendering, turning a maintainer's minimal-core stance into visible public work.

item.title: Hermes Makes Agent Approvals Thread-Local
item.description: Hermes replaced a shared approval flag with thread-local state, then paired that control with secret redaction and delegation-budget tests.

item.title: API Discovery: API Entreprise Makes Company Checks A Permissioned Agent Step
item.description: API Entreprise gives agents a public map for company checks, but the source also makes the permission boundary impossible to miss.

item.title: Codex Reset The Meter After Background Work Became User Work
item.description: OpenAI's public Codex usage-reset note reads like a customer-relief post. The public Codex repository shows something more specific: a cleanup across auto-review prompts, subagent delegation, review-time suggestions, and reset-credit visibility, while the usage backend remains outside the public diff.

item.title: OpenCode's Hardening Day Removes Layer Shortcuts
item.description: Agent Pulse saw OpenCode as a July 1 hardening signal, and the source receipts point to layer cleanup rather than launch noise.

item.title: Tau Lets Outside Contributors Shape The Exit Door
item.description: Tau's smallest command change became a public contributor story when an outside branch added /exit and the maintainer followed with contributing rules.

item.title: OpenClaw Narrows Who Can Wake An Agent
item.description: A cluster of OpenClaw commits put scheduled wakes, ACP runtime controls, and browser proxy invocation behind narrower target and scope checks.

item.title: API Discovery: API Geo Turns French Places Into Agent Inputs
item.description: The api.gouv.fr catalog records API Geo as an open public geography API with a technical definition, rate limit, WGS-84 coordinates, and GeoJSON support.

item.title: Crush Stops Writing To Vanished Shell Sessions
item.description: A public Crush patch moved shell-message persistence behind a session-existence check, turning a disappearing workspace edge case into tested agent reliability work.

item.title: Mario Zechner Kept Pi Compaction Visible
item.description: Two Pi coding-agent commits repaired the visible status and regression-test trail around interactive compaction, keeping context management inspectable instead of silent.

item.title: Tau Turns Agent Architecture Into Onboarding
item.description: A Tau documentation rewrite moved the coding-agent project from build-log internals toward a public quickstart, reference, package, and license surface.
